name: Red-Cooked Bean Curd, Family-Style
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the bean curd cake into 1-by-3-by-1-inch pieces.
2. Lay them on paper towels and drain for 10 minutes.
3. Cut the scallions into 3-inch pieces at a slight diagonal.
4. Heat a wok or large saute pan until hot.
5. Add the 1/2 cup of peanut oil, and when hot, fry the bean curd on both sides until golden brown.
6. Drain the bean curd well on paper towels. Discard the oil.
7. Wipe the wok clean, reheat, and add 1 1/2 cups of peanut oil.
8. Add scallions and garlic, and stir-fry for 30 seconds.
9. Put in the rest of the ingredients except the sesame oil.
10. Bring the mixture to a boil. Return the fried bean curd pieces and cook over high heat for 10 minutes or until the bean curd has absorbed most of the sauce.
11. Add the sesame oil, and give the mixture a final turn.
12. Serve at once.
